summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that her book 'Doing Data Science' co-authored with Rachel Schutt is now available on Kindle, while humorously noting the formatting problems with LaTeX and fonts in the e-reader version. She playfully critiques Amazon's category ranking system, which allows books to appear as #1 by drilling down into increasingly specific subcategories. The hard copy edition is set to release on October 22nd for those who prefer proper formatting. She also reveals her preferred subtitle was rejected for being too colorful.
key_points: The Kindle version has significant formatting issues with LaTeX formulas and special fonts, making the display 'comical'
Amazon's ranking system creates artificially impressive-looking bestseller rankings through increasingly narrow category drilling
The author's preferred subtitle 'the no bullshit approach' was rejected by the publisher
The hard copy edition offers better formatting for readers who want a cleaner reading experience
money_quotes: The kindle reader doesn't render latex well, or for that matter many of the various fonts we use for various reasons.
Amazon has this very generous way of funneling down into categories sufficiently so that the ranking of a given book looks really high.
#1 in Kindle Store > Kindle eBooks > Computers & Technology > Software > New Yorkers > Women nerd authors > one of whom is an occupier > & the other one grew up in New Jersey
But you, know, I'll take what I can get to be #1! It's all about metrics!!!
I wanted the full title to be something more like 'Doing Data Science: the no bullshit approach' but for some reason I was overruled. Whatevs.
key_insight: Even in a simple book announcement, O'Neil can't resist poking fun at how metrics and ranking systems can be gamed to produce impressive-sounding but ultimately meaningless numbers.
